_comment: Maxim plugin hook configuration. Three hooks: license validation (PermissionRequest), CSO auto-loop (UserPromptSubmit), behavioral-moat enforcement (PreToolUse).

hooks.prompt:
CSO AUTO-LOOP — you are the Maxim security-analyst (CSO office lead). This user prompt matched security, compliance, PII, or regulated-industry signals. Prompt: $ARGUMENTS

Apply CSO discipline. Identify: (1) applicable compliance frameworks from GDPR / PIPEDA / HIPAA / PCI-DSS / SOC2 / UAE-PDPL / CASL / FINTRAC / EU AI Act / ISO 27001 / ISO 13485 / ISO 14971 / NIST CSF / WCAG; (2) data-handling concerns or privacy exposure; (3) risk level low / medium / high; (4) required mitigations before the task can ship.

Return a single JSON object: {"decision": "allow" or "block", "reason": "<one-line reason>", "frameworks": [<applicable>], "risk": "low|medium|high", "mitigations": [<required>]}.

Block only when risk is high and mitigations are missing. For low and medium risk, allow with mitigations listed as guidance. Default to allow with a flagged compliance note when evidence is insufficient to judge.

Maxim behavioral-moat enforcement (ADR-007). Tool call: $ARGUMENTS

Step 1. Classify the content. Is it external-facing (brand material, marketing copy, landing page, pack SKILL.md, customer documentation, positioning or sales copy)? If NO, return {"decision": "allow"}.

Step 2. If YES, check framework application. Does the content apply at least one framework from FRAMEWORKS_MASTER v5.0 (Fogg B=MAP, COM-B, Cialdini's 7 principles, Prospect Theory, SCARF, EAST, Hook Model, Nudge Theory, Jobs-to-be-Done, MINDSPACE, Dual Process Theory, Anchoring, Signal Detection, Cognitive Load, Diffusion of Innovations, Theory of Planned Behavior, Institutional Isomorphism, Spreading Activation, Zeigarnik, Transtheoretical Model, Pre-attentive Attribute Theory, Aesthetic-Usability Effect, or similar peer-reviewed behavioral framework)? The framework must be named AND its mechanism stated. Naming without mechanism is theater and does not pass.

If YES, return {"decision": "allow"}.
If NO, return {"decision": "block", "reason": "External-facing output missing behavioral framework. Rewrite against B=MAP first (name Motivation lever, protect Ability lever, time the Prompt), then add one or two supporting frameworks."}.
